creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text: Whenever an underlying provider returns an error, gmultipath will fail that path and retry the command on another path, if any more remain.  So with n total paths, every failing command will be tried up to n times.  But some errors can't possibly be path-related.  For example, SCSI MEDIUM ERROR.  Those failures should be passed up the stack with no retries.
